steps.title: Identify exponential decay form
steps.content:
When a quantity decreases by a fixed percentage for each unit increase in $x$, the function has the form $q(x) = a \cdot b^x$.
A 45% decrease means the value retains 55% of its previous amount each time, so the base is $b = 0.55$.

The initial condition $q(0) = 14$ tells us that $a = 14$ (since $b^0 = 1$).

steps.title: Write the equation
steps.content: Substituting $a = 14$ and $b = 0.55$: $$q(x) = 14(0.55)^x$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.
